citations.summary: Linear covariance-root change of variables moves the localized fluctuation integral to the product standard Gaussian interface.
use_for: Source anchor for Gaussian pushforward after the covariance-root change of variables.
do_not_use_for: Do not treat this as the dictionary coordinate identification, determinant normalization, or root-localization theorem.
open_questions: Exact coordinate/dictionary statement for the repository's physical fluctuation field.
Normalization/Jacobian assumptions needed by the Lean Gaussian pushforward field.

citations.summary: Balaban localizes the product-Gaussian expression into quantities H(Z,Z0) and H(Z), with component factorization.
use_for: Source anchor for existence of localized activity H(Z) before Lemma 3 estimates.
do_not_use_for: Do not claim exact finite covariance-root reconstruction from this citation.
Do not claim the repository's active support inclusion without the enlargement convention.
open_questions: Exact map from Balaban localization domains to OmegaPolymerType supports.
Exact active-support and external-field locality theorem needed by Appendix-F support hypotheses.

citations.summary: Main resummation window for the activity H(Z), ending in Lemma 3 and the final exponential activity estimate.
use_for: Navigation to the exact source region for the remaining Lemma 3 boundary discharges.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite the OCR text as exact formula data.
Do not use this broad window as a single monolithic Lean hypothesis.
open_questions: Exact Eq. (2.29) display.
Exact Eq. (2.31) finite P-summation display.
Exact Eq. (2.37) post-P bound.
Exact C3 definition and constant restrictions.

citations.summary: Visually confirmed D-stage summability after the product estimate following (2.28): for K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small, the D-family product sum is at most one.
extracted_claims: Before Eq. (2.27), Balaban fixes one connected component Y0 and considers the product over Y in D in Eq. (2.26), extracting alpha6 * exp(-delta * kappa * d_k(Y)) from each factor.
Eq. (2.27) states sum_{Y in D} (d_k(Y) + 5) >= d_k(Y0) + 5, using that union_{Y in D} Y = Y0 and Y0 is connected.

Under the displayed smallness condition 2*E0*epsilon1*C1*alpha4^-1*alpha6^-1*M^q*exp(C2*kappa1)*exp(5*kappa) <= 1, Eq. (2.28) bounds the product from Eq. (2.26) by the Eq. (2.29) product times the remaining Y0 exponential prefactor.
Eq. (2.29) states sum_D product_{Y in D} alpha6 * exp(-delta * kappa * d_k(Y)) <= 1 for K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small.
Eq. (2.30) records the domain metric comparison (3*2^3)^-1 * M^-4 * |Y| <= d_k(Y) <= M^-4 * |Y| - 1 for localization domains Y in D_k.
use_for: Source anchor for the Eq. (2.29) D-family product, the product-sum bound, and the adjacent Eq. (2.27)/(2.30) metric comparisons.
do_not_use_for: Do not treat the qualitative phrase 'K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small' as a formal uniform threshold without extracting or proving the cited combinatorial argument.
Do not identify the repository's DIndex/DParts with Balaban's D-families until the source-to-Lean dictionary is supplied.
open_questions: Exact source-to-Lean dictionary for DIndex, DParts, and the localization-domain metric d_k.
Formal proof or source theorem for the large-K/small-alpha6 summability assertion, including threshold dependencies and uniformity over scale, volume, and background.
Exact role of the cited argument in [26] used to prove inequalities of this type.

citations.summary: Visual identification of the reference [26] cited by CMP116 Eq. (2.29)-type summability as C. Cammarota, Decay of correlations for infinite range interactions in unbounded spin systems, CMP 85, 517-528 (1982).
extracted_claims: CMP109 printed page 299, reference 26, lists C. Cammarota: Decay of correlations for infinite range interactions in unbounded spin systems, Communications in Mathematical Physics 85, 517-528 (1982).
CMP116 printed page 18 says inequalities of the Eq. (2.29) type can be proved by a simple modification of the argument in [26].
This citation identifies the external source to inspect next; it does not extract Cammarota's theorem, constants, hypotheses, or its translation to Balaban D-families.
use_for: Bibliographic source lookup for the external combinatorial/cluster-expansion argument behind CMP116 Eq. (2.29)-type product summability.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this entry as a theorem proving Eq. (2.29).
Do not infer threshold dependencies for K or alpha6 from this bibliographic identification alone.
Do not identify Cammarota's polymer/spin-system hypotheses with the repository's DIndex/DParts without extracting the actual theorem.
open_questions: Exact theorem, lemma, proposition, or equation in Cammarota CMP85 proving the polymer-family summability argument needed by Balaban.
Constants and threshold dependencies needed to convert Cammarota's argument into the qualitative CMP116 condition K sufficiently large and alpha6 sufficiently small.
Precise dictionary from Cammarota's interaction/polymer objects to Balaban's D-families and then to the repository's DIndex/DParts fields.

citations.summary: Primary bibliographic page located for Cammarota CMP85, the external argument CMP116 cites for Eq. (2.29)-type summability; the exact theorem, constants, hypotheses, and Balaban D-family dictionary are still not extracted.
extracted_claims: Springer confirms the article metadata: C. Cammarota, Communications in Mathematical Physics 85, 517-528 (1982), DOI 10.1007/BF01403502.
The Springer abstract says the paper uses an associated polymer model and gives a proof of convergence of the Mayer series for the general polymer model.
Project Euclid advertises a PDF endpoint for this article, but the automation environment received an anti-bot HTML page rather than a PDF.
ResearchGate exposes an author-uploaded OCR/full-text view, but it is not yet a local primary PDF artifact and its OCR is too corrupted to theorem-feed Eq. (2.29).
use_for: Precise source request and access ledger for the Cammarota theorem behind CMP116 Eq. (2.29)-type D-family summability.
do_not_use_for: Do not cite this entry as a theorem proving Eq. (2.29).
Do not use ResearchGate OCR to set Lean constants or thresholds.
Do not identify Cammarota polymers with Balaban D-families or repository DIndex/DParts until the exact theorem and dictionary are extracted.
open_questions: Exact printed theorem/lemma/proposition/equation in Cammarota CMP85 that proves the polymer-family summability used by Balaban.
Exact smallness hypothesis, constants, metric/diameter convention, dimension dependence, and uniformity in volume.
Exact route from Cammarota's polymer-model theorem to Balaban's qualitative large-K/small-alpha6 Eq. (2.29) assertion.
Exact source-to-Lean dictionary from Cammarota polymers to Balaban D-families and then to DIndex/DParts.

citations.summary: Visually confirmed Eq. (2.31) P-summation with fixed Z0 and Y0, controlled by the exponential factor involving the finite bond set P.
extracted_claims: For fixed Z0 and Y0, the displayed P-sum has factors exp(-rho * M^-4 * |Z0 \ Y0|) and exp(-2 * rho * |P|), where rho = (1/20) * gamma2 * epsilon1^2 / gk^2.
The displayed powerset/cardinality bound is exp(-M^-4 * |Z0 \ Y0| * (rho - 4 * M^4 * exp(-2 * rho))) <= 1.
The localization/counting scale visible in Eq. (2.31) is M, and the Lean gapMass abstraction corresponds to M^-4 * |Z0 \ Y0|.
The source text immediately before Eq. (2.31) describes P as a set of bonds and says |P| >= (1/2) * M^-4 * |Z0 \ Y0| because one bond in P may connect two cubes in Z0 \ Y0.
use_for: Source anchor for the Eq. (2.31) rate rho, gapMass shape, exact bracket condition rho - 4*M^4*exp(-2*rho), and finite bond-subset entropy bound.
do_not_use_for: Do not assert the repository's pBonds_injective, pBonds_subset, or PIndex dictionary until the exact source-to-Lean set map is supplied.
Do not cite the stronger Lean sufficient condition 80*M^4*gk^2 <= gamma2*epsilon1^2 as a verbatim source hypothesis.
open_questions: Exact dictionary from Balaban's finite bond set P to the repository's PIndex/pBonds fields.
Formal source proof of the carrier/cardinality bound used as bondCarrier_card_le in Lean.
Exact parameter-hierarchy statement relating gk, gamma, gamma2, epsilon1, M, and kappa after Eq. (2.31).

citations.summary: Source-targeting entry for the exact Balaban P-family, eligible bond carrier, injective encoding, and carrier-count theorem needed before replacing arbitrary CMP116Eq231PBondBoundary hypotheses.
extracted_claims: The visually confirmed Eq. (2.31) display fixes the rate rho, the gap factor M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0|, and the powerset/cardinality bracket used by the current finite-sum theorem.
The visually confirmed paragraph describes P as a finite bond set and gives the lower bound |P| >= (1/2)*M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0|.

The inspected citation material does not yet give a formal source-to-Lean map from Balaban's P to repository PIndex/pBonds, nor does it prove the carrier upper bound recorded by CMP116Eq231PBondBoundary.
use_for: Precise source request for removing the live arbitrary Eq. (2.31) bond-boundary hypothesis from source-facing P-stage and M3 routes.
Checklist for the source facts required to construct repository PIndex/pBonds from Balaban's finite bond sets.
do_not_use_for: Do not construct a source-native PIndex, pBonds_injective, pBonds_subset, gapMass, or bondCarrier_card_le theorem from this entry alone.
Do not treat the source lower bound on |P| as the carrier upper bound needed by Lean.
Do not replace CMP116Eq231PBondBoundary with a record that merely repackages the same arbitrary data under a source name.
open_questions: Exact definition of the eligible bond universe for fixed Z0 and Y0, including orientation and adjacency conventions.
Whether Balaban's P is literally a subset of that eligible carrier or an encoded object requiring a quotient/dictionary.
Proof that the source P-family has an injective representation as finite bond subsets in the repository convention.
Proof of the carrier count |Carrier(Z0,Y0)| <= 4*|Z0 \ Y0|, equivalently <= 4*M^4*gapMass under the Lean gapMass convention.
Exact relationship between the source lower bound |P| >= (1/2)*M^-4*|Z0 \ Y0| and the repository weight exp(-2*rho*|P|).

citations.summary: Post-P resummation over D, P, and Z0 families leading to the fixed Z0' bound before the final Z0' summation.
use_for: Source target for the combined post-P source boundary.
do_not_use_for: Do not split this into standalone Z0 and Z0' residual theorems unless the source text explicitly supports that split.
open_questions: Whether the Lean post-P source boundary is sourced from (2.37) plus the following paragraph or only from Lemma 3/(2.38).
Exact majorization into the canonical Lemma-3 base factor.

citations.summary: Definition region for alpha5 restrictions and the final C3 constant used in Lemma 3.
use_for: Source target for the constant hierarchy request.
do_not_use_for: Do not collapse repeated O(1) constants into one Lean constant without a source majorant theorem.
open_questions: Exact definitions of epsilon1, epsilon2, gamma2, alpha0, alpha1, alpha4, alpha5, alpha6, and C3.
Whether distinct O(1) constants need distinct Lean fields or a common majorant.

citations.summary: Final Lemma 3 activity estimate for H(Z), after all restrictions and resummations.
use_for: Final target shape of the Lemma 3 activity estimate.
do_not_use_for: Do not use (2.38) to pretend the upstream Eq. (2.31) or post-P source boundaries have been proved separately.
open_questions: Exact exponent convention: OCR and existing notes differ on visible half-power/one-half formatting.
Full restriction list preceding Lemma 3.

citations.summary: Post-Lemma-3 insertion of the H(Z) bound into the effective-action estimate.
use_for: Downstream effective-action source anchor after Lemma 3 is available.
do_not_use_for: Do not use this as a proof of the Lemma 3 resummation itself.
open_questions: Exact Lean theorem target for the post-Lemma-3 effective-action consumer.
